title: Townsendia leptotes
taxonomy: Townsendia leptotes is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the sunflower or daisy family. It is a member of the genus Townsendia, which is a group of small, shrubby plants native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. Townsendia leptotes is further classified as a member of the subgenus Townsendia, within the genus. The species name leptotes is derived from the Greek words "leptos," meaning slender, and "otes," meaning leaf. As a member of the Asteraceae family, Townsendia leptotes is closely related to other plants such as daisies, sunflowers, and coneflowers.

intro: Townsendia leptotes, also known as Fringed Townsendia, is a flowering plant in the daisy family native to the western United States. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiparasitic effects, which may help to soothe skin irritations and support the immune system. The bioactive constituents of Fringed Townsendia include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpene lactones, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Townsendia leptotes is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and salves, which are commonly used to support skin health and alleviate symptoms of minor wounds and infections.
